collected_evidence.summary: Progress dashboard UI/UX evidence passed browser-rendered responsive, state, and escaping checks.

source_influences.evidence_rule: Accessibility, touch, performance, style, responsive layout, typography, animation, forms, navigation, and data must be inspectable.

source_influences.role: local-first design system and sandboxed preview/evidence pattern
source_influences.evidence_rule: UI evidence should be generated from local project files and written back as artifacts.
Agents need a durable text design source, not only screenshots or chat memory.

source_influences.role: design resource taxonomy for colors, icons, information architecture, and user research
source_influences.evidence_rule: Design evidence should name the checked domains instead of relying on vague polish language.
UI evidence collectors should stay adapter-based and replaceable, not hardcoded to one tool.

checks.message: Dashboard HTML must declare a mobile viewport.
Active run UI must expose progress, stats, task list, review, and sidebar regions.
Idle UI must still show lifecycle state and evidence count.
Dashboard must include mobile and desktop responsive rules.
Server-rendered task and gate text must be escaped before entering HTML.
Client-side SSE re-render must escape task text and avoid raw selector interpolation.
UI/UX evidence must carry design contract, 6-pillar review, and quality-domain metadata.
Task data must not be able to create executable inline event handlers.
Chrome headless must render desktop dashboard evidence without crashing.
